content.problemStatement:
Bitland declared war on Chefland and sent an army to fight them, but Chefland defended efficiently and Bitland's army has been reduced to NNN soldiers. They have no chance of winning the war and do not want to surrender, so they are planning to commit group suicide. Josh, the leader of Bitland's remaining soldiers, has different plans — he wants to survive and somehow escape.

The soldiers are numbered 111 through NNN; Josh is soldier NNN. The soldiers are going to stand in a circle in the order 1,2,…,P−1,N,P,P+1,…,N−11, 2, \ldots, P-1, N, P, P+1, \ldots, N-11,2,…,P−1,N,P,P+1,…,N−1. Formally, they are standing in the circle in such a way that if Josh's position is PPP (1≤P≤N1 \le P \le N1≤P≤N), then for each iii (1≤i≤N−21 \le i \le N-21≤i≤N−2, i≠P−1i \neq P-1i=P−1), soldier i+1i+1i+1 is directly to the right of soldier iii, soldier PPP (if P≤N−1P \le N-1P≤N−1) or 111 (if P=NP = NP=N) is directly to the right of Josh and Josh is directly to the right of soldier P−1P-1P−1 (if P≥2P \ge 2P≥2) or soldier N−1N-1N−1 (if P=1P = 1P=1); if 2≤P≤N−22 \le P \le N-22≤P≤N−2, soldier 111 is also directly to the right of soldier N−1N-1N−1. For each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), soldier iii has a sword with power AiA_iAi​. Josh plans to take a shield with sufficiently high defense power DDD.

First, the soldiers start to commit group suicide according to the following rules:


Initially, soldier 111 is the attacking soldier.
If the attacking soldier is not Josh, this soldier attacks the soldier that is currently to his right.
When Josh is attacked with power aaa, the current defense power of his shield decreases by aaa, and if it becomes negative, Josh dies. When a different soldier is attacked, he does not try to defend and dies immediately. The power of the attacking soldier's sword does not change.
Then, the next living soldier to the right of the current attacking soldier becomes the attacking soldier and the process continues until there is only one soldier left.
It is clear that this way, Josh cannot be the last survivor. However, Chefland's general Chef plans to interrupt this process as soon as there are exactly two living soldiers of Bitland left (Josh wants to be one of them) by attacking them with Chefland's full firepower FFF. Since this attack is unexpected, both attacked soldiers try to defend independently with the weapons they have. Josh survives if the current defense power of his shield is at least FFF. Any other soldier survives only if the power of his sword is strictly greater than FFF. Since Chefland does not attack again, if both Josh and another soldier survive, then the other soldier will kill Josh. Therefore, Josh wants to be the only survivor of Chefland's attack.

Your task is to find the minimum possible value of DDD such that Josh survives if he chooses his position PPP optimally (or determine that he cannot survive) and the lowest position PPP such that Josh survives if he takes a shield with this defense power DDD.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,000,0051,000,0051,000,005
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers A1,A2,…,AN−1A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{N-1}A1​,A2​,…,AN−1​.
The third line contains a single integer FFF.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, first, print a line containing the string `"possible"` if Josh can survive or `"impossible"` if he cannot (without quotes). Then, if he can survive, print a second line containing two space-separated integers PPP and DDD.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: When Josh is at the position P=4P = 4P=4, the soldiers' initial powers in clockwise order around the circle, starting with soldier 111, are [12,34,45,D,5][12, 34, 45, D, 5][12,34,45,D,5] (DDD denotes Josh). Then, the following happens:

The soldier with power 121212 attacks the soldier with power 343434 and kills him.
The soldier with power 454545 attacks Josh, who defends. The living soldiers' powers are now [12,45,D−45,5][12, 45, D-45, 5][12,45,D−45,5] and Josh is the attacking soldier.
Josh does not attack, so the soldier to his right (with power 555) becomes the attacking soldier. He attacks the soldier with power 121212 and kills him.
The soldier with power 454545 attacks Josh again and Josh defends again.
The soldier with power 555 attacks the soldier with power 454545 and kills him.
Now, two soldiers are left: Josh (with a shield with defense power D−90D-90D−90) and the soldier with a sword with power 555. Each of them is attacked with firepower F=10F=10F=10, so the power of Josh's shield drops to D−100D-100D−100 and the other soldier dies.

If Josh survives, his shield's initial power DDD should be at least 45+45+10=10045+45+10 = 10045+45+10=100. For all other positions PPP, Josh cannot survive.
Example case 2: Regardless of how large DDD is and which position Josh chooses, after Chefland's attack, a soldier of Bitland other than Josh will always survive. This soldier will then attack Josh until his shield breaks and he dies.
